channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Claude Code on Microsoft Foundry in VS Code — A Practical Setup Guide (with the gotchas)
item.description: Enables enterprise-grade governance without changing your developer workflow. The official Microsoft Learn article (Configure Claude Code for Microsoft Foundry) gets you ~80% of the way there. The remaining 20%—VS Code settings shape, tenant mismatches, and configuration conflicts like "baseURL and...
item.dc:creator: LZhang

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Sentinel: May 2026
item.description: Welcome to the May edition of What's new in Microsoft Sentinel. This month’s updates focus on unified role-based access control (RBAC), ecosystem breadth, AI-agent security, and high-assurance identity. RBAC and row-level scoping are now generally available, giving security teams a single, granular...
item.dc:creator: Sowmys

item.title: The Azure Copilot Observability Agent Chat - Stop Writing Queries, Start Asking Questions.
item.description: Services and applications produce massive amounts of telemetry – and making sense of all this data takes effort. Data is often spread across different stores, which means the way to clear insights goes through careful querying, refinement and correlation. The Azure Copilot Observability agent now...
item.dc:creator: Noa Kuperberg

item.title: Agents That Build Agents: A SKILL-first Blueprint with MS Agent Framework & Foundry
item.description: The single insight that changes everything Most "build an AI agent" tutorials collapse two completely different jobs into one tangled mess: the job of building an agent (writing the code, defining its tools, evaluating it, packaging it), and the job of running an agent (planning, reasoning, calling...
item.dc:creator: kinfey

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Foundry | May 2026
item.description: May ships trace-based evaluation for any agent on any cloud, Grok 4.3 and DeepSeek V4 in the model catalog, GPT-5 Reinforcement Fine-Tuning at gated GA, three Microsoft Research on-device agent models, Managed VNET at GA, project-level cost attribution, Content Understanding improvements reaching...
item.dc:creator: Nick Brady

item.title: Cannot install addins
item.description: I am a Microsoft 365 Personal user. When I try to add the Claude for Excel Addin (for the Desktop version of Excel), I get an error: "Error loading add-ins. One or more add-ins failed to load. See your add-ins.". However, this is not specific to the Claude addin, since every addin gives the same...
item.dc:creator: pavanmaverick

item.title: Malicious npm packages abuse dependency confusion to profile developer environments
item.description: A dependency confusion campaign leveraged 33 malicious npm packages to collect reconnaissance data from developer and build environments. This report details the attack chain, observed tradecraft, and detection opportunities to help organizations identify and disrupt related activity. The post...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Azure Functions MCP extension now supports MCP Prompts
item.description: We are thrilled to announce that the MCP prompt trigger is now available in public preview in the Azure Functions MCP extension! With this release, the extension now supports all three core MCP server primitives - tools, resources, and prompts, giving you a complete platform for building rich MCP...
item.dc:creator: lily-ma

item.title: Copilot usage metrics API adds cohorts for AI adoption
item.description: To help you tell a deeper Copilot adoption story—not just who is active, but how they’re using Copilot—the Copilot usage metrics API now classifies each engaged user into an AI… The post Copilot usage metrics API adds cohorts for AI adoption appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Flexible Cooling for AI Growth: How Zonal Architecture Supports Diverse Hardware Needs
item.description: By: Ricardo Bianchini, Steve Solomon, Brijesh Warrier, Martin Herbert, Jay Jochim, Husam Alissa, Pulkit Misra, Eric Peterson and Cam Turner Context - Microsoft is pioneering zonal cooling in its next-generation AI datacenters, enabling flexible, performant, efficient, and sustainable thermal...
item.dc:creator: stsolo

item.title: Four open source projects to explore at Microsoft Build
item.description: Open source is where developers experiment, collaborate, and turn new ideas into tools that others can build on. At Microsoft Build, we’re creating a dedicated space for that energy: the Open Source Zone. This year, the Open Source Zone will bring together maintainers, contributors, and developers...
item.dc:creator: leereilly

item.title: Fabric Influencers Spotlight: May 2026
item.description: Welcome to the May 2026 edition of the Fabric Influencers Spotlight, a recurring monthly post here to shine a bright light on the places on the internet where Microsoft MVPs and Fabric Super Users are doing some amazing work on all aspects of Microsoft Fabric. The Microsoft Fabric Community team...
item.dc:creator: mamathews

item.title: Microsoft is named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Endpoint Protection
item.description: Microsoft is named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Endpoint Protection. The post Microsoft is named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Endpoint Protection appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Rob Lefferts

item.title: Add Security Key Support to Microsoft Authenticator and Managed Home Screen
item.description: undefined
item.dc:creator: AbeSummers

item.title: KB5089573 forced install + Lenovo network stack degradation (HTTPS latency, build 26200.8524)
item.description: KB5089573 installed automatically on a Lenovo system despite updates being paused and no preview updates enabled. After installation, the system jumped to build 26200.8524 and the network stack degraded severely. Heavy HTTPS sites (LinkedIn, Google Finance, YouTube) take 20–60 seconds to load...
item.dc:creator: phermi

item.title: Microsoft Cans Power BI App for Reporting Microsoft 365 Usage
item.description: Microsoft has announced that the Microsoft 365 Usage Analytics Power BI app will retire on August 1, 2026. The alternative is the usage reports in the Microsoft 365 admin center or to develop a custom Power BI dashboard based on data imported through the Graph usage reports API. The decision isn’t...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Edge displays a splash screen saying ‘Sign in to sync your data’
item.description: HelloWhen the user logs in to a device for the first time and launches Edge, the following splash screen appears, even though we have created the Intune configuration below, which is intended to prevent this. We have following Intune configuration: Why does the splash screen still appear?
item.dc:creator: staeheli

item.title: Power Pages & SPA: Deploying a Custom React SPA to Microsoft Power Pages
item.description: Introduction Modern business applications are no longer limited to traditional server-rendered pages or fixed portal layouts. Today’s users expect fast, responsive, highly interactive web experiences - the kind of experience developers commonly build using modern front-end frameworks such as React....
item.dc:creator: aakarshdhawan

item.title: From Requirement to Production Code, How Engineering Squad Automates the Full Dev Lifecycle
item.description: I started wondering: what if instead of one AI assistant generating code snippets, you had an entire squad of specialized AI agents. Each owning a single stage of the delivery pipeline, they could collaborate, self-correct, and produce a complete, traceable output from a plain-text requirement?...
item.dc:creator: paggarwal

item.title: Azure DDoS Protection & Azure WAF: A Layered Defense for Modern DDoS Attacks
item.description: Introduction: The Need for Layered DDoS Defense Organizations today operate in an environment where Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks continue to evolve across both network and application layers. To help organizations build resilient, internet-facing applications and services, Microsoft...
item.dc:creator: saikishor

item.title: Training 100B+ Models on a Single GPU: What MegaTrain Changes - and What It Means for Azure
item.description: The Paradigm Shift in Model Training The conventional wisdom in deep learning has been simple: bigger models require bigger infrastructure. Training a 100-billion parameter language model traditionally demands massive GPU clusters with terabytes of combined memory, where each GPU holds portions of...
item.dc:creator: yuvmaz

item.title: Typosquatted npm packages used to steal cloud and CI/CD secrets
item.description: The Mini Shai-Hulud campaign used malicious npm packages to target cloud and CI/CD credentials across developer environments. This report details the attack chain, detection opportunities, and mitigation guidance to help organizations identify and disrupt related activity. The post Typosquatted npm...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: [Preview] CIS Benchmarks on Azure; Now for Windows Server
item.description: For customers with machines managed by Azure (i.e., Arc-enabled machines and Azure VMs), last year we delivered built-in CIS Benchmarks for Linux. The feedback has been clear: “Excellent, now do Windows.” Today we're announcing that built-in CIS Benchmarks for Windows Server are coming to Azure...
item.dc:creator: AmirB

item.title: Incremental Liquid Clustering in Microsoft Fabric: Faster, smarter, and truly incremental
item.description: Miles Cole — Principal Program Manager, Microsoft
item.dc:creator: milescole-msft

item.title: Governing AI Agents Against Every OWASP Agentic Risk: A Deep Dive with the Agent Governance Toolkit
item.description: AI agents are moving from prototypes to production. They book flights, write code, negotiate contracts, and operate across enterprise systems with minimal human oversight. The attack surface is not theoretical: OWASP has catalogued the top 10 risks specific to agentic applications, and every one of...
item.dc:creator: mosiddi

item.title: Hard budget limits now available for GitHub Advanced Security
item.description: Enterprise administrators and billing managers can now set hard budget limits for GitHub Advanced Security (GHAS) SKUs, preventing teams from exceeding their allocated license budgets. Previously, license-based products like GHAS… The post Hard budget limits now available for GitHub Advanced...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: CodeQL 2.25.5 improves query accuracy for GitHub Actions
item.description: CodeQL is the static analysis engine behind GitHub code scanning, which finds and remediates security issues in your code. We’ve recently released CodeQL 2.25.5, which includes accuracy improvements across C/C++,… The post CodeQL 2.25.5 improves query accuracy for GitHub Actions appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: No option to go to message from search without opening side panel
item.description: When I search for a term (whether using All or Messages) and click on a result, I intend to go to that message in the chat or direct message with the whole window, just as if I had navigated there manually. I do NOT want to open an awkward side panel with that conversation. There are no right click...
item.dc:creator: BobFromIT

item.title: [Now Generally Available] Customizable Security Baseline Policies in Machine Configuration!
item.description: Background: Azure Machine Configuration remains committed to enabling greater security and simplicity in at-scale server management for all Azure customers. Machine Configuration (previously known as Azure Policy Guest Configuration) enables both built-in and custom configuration as code allowing...
item.dc:creator: mutemwamasheke

item.title: Who's Calling Your Service? Designing for Humans and Agents at the Same Time
item.description: We're building three interfaces for Azure SRE Agent: an interactive CLI for humans at a terminal, an agent mode for coding agents that spawn it as a subprocess, and an MCP server for humans inside coding agents and for remote agents in other ecosystems. The CLI and agent mode are coming. The MCP...
item.dc:creator: dbandaru

item.title: Still a developer. Just outside. Our latest GitHub Shop collection is here.
item.description: The ESC collection lets you escape the confines of your desk and get out into the sun where good ideas are bound to happen. The post Still a developer. Just outside. Our latest GitHub Shop collection is here. appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Ansible + Azure Arc: Manage Azure Arc Extensions with New Ansible Modules
item.description: We’re excited to announce new modules in Ansible Galaxy that make it easier to manage Azure Arc machine extensions at scale. With the latest updates to the azure.azcollection on Ansible Galaxy, you can now deploy and manage Azure Arc extensions using familiar, declarative Ansible workflows. These...
item.dc:creator: alinetran

item.title: SharePoint Framework (SPFx) roadmap update – May 2026
item.description: SPFx is powering the future of Microsoft 365 with AI driven portals and deep integrations across SharePoint and Microsoft 365. The May 2026 update shares new features and the roadmap for the next evolution built for the AI era. This is a transparent community driven journey that we shape together...
item.dc:creator: Vesa Juvonen

item.title: [Public Preview] Introducing Customizable Security Baseline Policies in Machine Configuration
item.description: Background: Azure Machine Configuration remains committed to enabling greater security and simplicity in at-scale server management for all Azure customers. Machine Configuration (previously known as Azure Policy Guest Configuration) enables both built-in and custom configuration as code allowing...
item.dc:creator: mutemwamasheke

item.title: Improve your agentic developer tools by grounding in Microsoft Learn
item.description: Development workflows span terminals, IDEs, background agents, and custom assistants. What matters is whether they draw from the same current source. Learn MCP Server gives any MCP-compatible agent direct access to current Microsoft documentation – one endpoint, nothing to install, no...
item.dc:creator: Pieter de Bruin

item.title: How Azure Chaos Studio ships with hermetic Aspire end-to-end tests
item.description: Learn how the Azure Chaos Studio team uses Aspire.Hosting.Testing to run hermetic, per-PR end-to-end tests across four services — with emulators, stubs, and no shared environments. The post How Azure Chaos Studio ships with hermetic Aspire end-to-end tests appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: Brent Rusinow

item.title: Claude Opus 4.8 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description: Claude Opus 4.8, Anthropic’s latest Opus model, is now available in GitHub Copilot. In our early testing, Opus 4.8 demonstrates a clear step forward in code understanding and generation across… The post Claude Opus 4.8 is generally available for GitHub Copilot appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: The Gentlemen ransomware: Dissecting a self-propagating Go encryptor
item.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence presents a comprehensive analysis of The Gentlemen, a Go-based ransomware deployed by affiliates of Storm-2697 that combines per-file ephemeral key encryption with an aggressive self-propagation module to deploy itself across an entire network using series of...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Threat Intelligence

item.title: Migration from Hosted Exchange (Hybrid) to M365 Classic Outlook Client Problems and Solutions
item.description: Hello Everyone,I'm a tech who started on a 8088 processor in the 80's. Not mentioning the Vic20 and C64 since that hardly seem relevant!I'm posting here to hopefully help the next person with the issues I've had over the last few weeks. My client had to port his email from a provider with an...
item.dc:creator: pinkish_panther

item.title: SQL + AI, hands-on: Join a free workshop near you
item.description: If you work with Microsoft SQL regularly, the AI conversation right now probably feels a little exhausting. Every week brings a new platform, a new pattern, a new opinion about how you’re “supposed” to build AI apps. Most of it assumes you’ll start over. You don’t have to. We’re running SQL AI App...
item.dc:creator: Anna Hoffman

item.title: Broken functionality of macOSWiFiConfiguration policies
item.description: I'm having trouble accessing macOSWiFiConfiguration policies. They are completely inaccessible via the Intune admin portal (no actual data is displayed) and the Microsoft Graph API. When using Graph (/beta/deviceManagement/deviceConfigurations or with policyId) an InternalServerError is returned...
item.dc:creator: MikolajKornas

item.title: Microsoft Rolls Out the New Calendar Sharing Model
item.description: An automatic upgrade is currently ongoing to move Outlook calendar sharing from the old MAPI-based model to a new REST-based model. The new model has been available to Outlook classic clients on an optional basis and is now becoming the default. The REST model is simpler and exploits the Exchange...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Verification-driven tooling prompts for fast-moving codebases
item.description: A practical pattern for using GenAI research agents to produce reliable internal tooling by verifying assumptions with detection classes and a final checklist. The post Verification-driven tooling prompts for fast-moving codebases appeared first on ISE Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Dexter Williams

item.title: Building Agentic Systems on Azure: Microsoft Foundry Agents SDK vs Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description: In my recent experience as a Senior Consultant at Microsoft, I’ve been actively involved in designing and delivering AI-driven solutions, with a strong focus on building intelligent agents using modern frameworks. Along the way, I've built agents using both Microsoft Foundry Agents SDK (hereafter...
item.dc:creator: ChaitanyaThalloory

item.title: Access Your SRE Agent from Any IDE, Terminal, or AI Assistant
item.description: Your team already uses an SRE Agent — it monitors your services, learns your architecture, and handles operational tasks. Now developers can talk to that agent in natural language from the interfaces they already use every day: their editor, their terminal, their AI assistant. Check what the agent...
item.dc:creator: dbandaru

item.title: preview builds prevents virus and threat protections to start
item.description: I have win11Pro, upgraded from 10 home. I am an insider and have switched to a canary channel. I never switched it, but now I only get the preview release builds. I am trying to download some trial software for my work and cannot since it "fails virus scanning." When i checked on the virus and...
item.dc:creator: manodwell

item.title: I’m Starting a New Cosmos DB App. What Security Do I Actually Need?
item.description: You just created a Cosmos DB account. The portal handed you two keys and a connection string, it worked, and you moved on. That’s what most developers do, and it causes problems later. This post is a guide for developers launching a new Cosmos DB app who want a secure default setup without...
item.dc:creator: Sudhanshu Khera, Iria Osara

item.title: Inspektor Gadget Completes Its First Independent Security Audit
item.description: One thing I've learned working with Open Source software over the years is that the projects you can trust most are the ones willing to let someone else test and review. That's what's recently happened with Inspektor Gadget, the open source eBPF tool for Kubernetes observability and Linux host...
item.dc:creator: Brian Benz

item.title: How AI coding agents actually use your technology
item.description: You ship an SDK, a CLI, an API, and developers use it. Now AI coding agents use it too, except they use it differently than humans do. Most of the time you have no idea what’s actually happening between “developer types a prompt” and “agent generates code with your technology.” Is the agent reading...
item.dc:creator: Waldek Mastykarz

item.title: What was Windows XP for you?
item.description: Almost everybody has heard of the legendary operating system Windows XP, but what was Windows XP specifically for you? Nostalgia, childhood?
item.dc:creator: CTCSD
